name: Venous Thromboembolism - Diagnosis Code Query (DVT + PE)
description: Tests ability to find VTE patients via diagnosis codes. VTE encompasses two main entities: deep venous thrombosis (DVT — most commonly in proximal lower extremity veins) and pulmonary embolism (PE). The Synthea module distributes patients across DVT (parent), iliofemoral DVT (extent-specific), inferior vena cava thrombosis, PE, and venous embolism (NOS). An LLM querying only one code will miss substantial fraction.
prompt: Find all patients with a diagnosis of venous thromboembolism, including deep vein thrombosis (DVT) of the lower extremities, iliofemoral DVT, inferior vena cava thrombosis, and pulmonary embolism (PE).

prompts.naive: Find all patients with blood clots.
prompts.broad: Find all patients diagnosed with any form of venous thromboembolism (VTE) — including deep vein thrombosis of lower extremities (proximal DVT, popliteal/femoral/iliofemoral), inferior vena cava thrombosis, and pulmonary embolism (PE).
prompts.expert: I need to identify all VTE patients. VTE is documented under multiple SNOMED codes from the Joint Commission VTE value set (OID 2.16.840.1.113883.3.117.1.7.1.330): Deep venous thrombosis (128053003) is the parent DVT concept, with extent-specific variants Iliofemoral DVT (234044007) and Inferior vena cava thrombosis (281595001). Pulmonary embolism (59282003) is the parent PE concept, and Venous embolism (234049002) is a NOS catch-all. Search Condition resources for all five codes.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with VTE diagnosis codes (Paths A and B). Tests anatomic-extent and DVT-vs-PE code variation.
